title: Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: The Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Aru Valley, Pahalgam near Anantnag city in Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: The Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Aru Valley, Pahalgam near Anantnag city in Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It lies 46 km from Anantnag city, the district headquarter. It is on the periphery of the two villages of Overa and Aru. The sanctuary spreads over 511 square kilometres (197 mi2), lies 76 kilometres (47 mi) east of Srinagar.

topPlaces.name: Aru, Jammu and Kashmir
topPlaces.description: Aru, known as Adav in Kashmiri, is a village, tourist spot in the Anantnag District of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is 53 km from Anantnag city, the district headquarter.

topPlaces.name: Lidder River
topPlaces.description: The Lidder or Liddar (IPA: /lʲədɨr/) is a 73 km (45 mi) river situated in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It originates from the Kolahoi Glacier and feeds the Jhelum River in Mirgund Khanabal, at an altitude of 1,615 metres (5,299 ft).

topPlaces.name: Mount Kolahoi
topPlaces.description: Kolahoi Peak (locally called 'Gashe-braed' meaning Illuminated Cat) is a mountain with peak elevation of 5,425 metres (17,799 ft) located in Lidder Valley, Jammu and Kashmir. Kolahoi Peak is easily accessible through Aru Pahalgam.

topPlaces.name: Tarsar Lake
topPlaces.description: The Tarsar Lake or Tar Sar is an almond-shaped, oligotrophic alpine lake situated in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir. On its west side lies Dachigam National Park, and on its south side lies Tral.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Aru, Jammu and Kashmir & around

items.note: Aru, known as Adav in Kashmiri, is a village, tourist spot in the Anantnag District of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is 53 km from Anantnag city, the district headquarter.

The Lidder or Liddar (IPA: /lʲədɨr/) is a 73 km (45 mi) river situated in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It originates from the Kolahoi Glacier and feeds the Jhelum River in Mirgund Khanabal, at an altitude of 1,615 metres (5,299 ft).

Kolahoi Peak (locally called 'Gashe-braed' meaning Illuminated Cat) is a mountain with peak elevation of 5,425 metres (17,799 ft) located in Lidder Valley, Jammu and Kashmir. Kolahoi Peak is easily accessible through Aru Pahalgam.

The Tarsar Lake or Tar Sar is an almond-shaped, oligotrophic alpine lake situated in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir. On its west side lies Dachigam National Park, and on its south side lies Tral.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (128 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aru, Jammu and Kashmir, Lidder River, Mount Kolahoi, Tarsar Lake.
